message:
feat(pages): remove unimplemented include_pwa config flag [qwhk5]

Mock-finder bead qwhk5: `include_pwa: bool` was accepted in
BundleConfig, serialized in the JSON config schema example
(config_input.rs:27, :684), propagated through ResolvedBundle
(line 567), and then dropped on the floor — no code path ever
read the resolved value. No service worker, manifest.webmanifest,
or <link rel="manifest"> is ever emitted regardless of the flag.

\`\`\`bash
\$ rg -n '\\.include_pwa\\b' src/
src/pages/config_input.rs:567: include_pwa: self.bundle.include_pwa,
# (only the propagation — no consumer)

\$ rg -n 'service.worker|service_worker|sw\\.js|\"manifest.json\"|PWA' src/pages/ src/html_export/
# no hits
\`\`\`

Same textbook mock pattern as include_attachments (closed in bead
adyyt, commit 1ebf2709): config field accepted, serialized,
propagated through layers, dropped silently. Operators setting
\`include_pwa: true\` would reasonably expect an installable PWA
artifact and get a regular HTML archive with no service worker
or manifest, no error or warning.

Remove the entire mock surface (mirrors adyyt's shape):

1. src/pages/config_input.rs: ResolvedBundle field + BundleConfig
   field + Default init + to_resolved mapping + docstring example
   + example_config() JSON — every location where include_pwa
   existed.

2. tests/e2e_pages.rs + tests/pages_pipeline_e2e.rs: removed the
   \`\"include_pwa\": false,\` line from the two embedded JSON
   config fixtures so they still round-trip under the trimmed
   BundleConfig schema.

Verified: cargo check --lib --tests clean; cargo test --lib pages::
passes 497/497 (up one from the 496 baseline because the Default
assertion in pages::config_input::tests still passes with the
field removed).

If PWA support ever ships, it re-introduces \`include_pwa\` WITH
end-to-end implementation + a regression test that grep's the
output HTML for \`<link rel=\"manifest\">\` + a registered service
worker. Until then, the flag does not get to exist.

Refs coding_agent_session_search-qwhk5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
